transcript.text: What does this project do and who uses it?
Embeddable chat widget SDK. Customers drop it into their own page via <script src> from our CDN or npm install — it's a browser-direct-js SDK, not a hosted app we control.

What's the deployment situation — does this run in the customer's page directly, or behind an iframe boundary? I see <script src> usage in the README and a destroy() teardown method in ChatWidget, but detect_project has no field for confirming unsandboxed embedding.
Directly in the customer's page, no iframe. browser-direct-js — there's no sandbox, so instance isolation and destroy() cleanup are load-bearing.
Who's the audience, and is there a compliance regime we should hold this to?
Enterprise customers embed this on production marketing and support pages. No SOC2/HIPAA/PCI requirement today — it's a stateless widget, no payment or health data ever touches it.
Is this project AI-powered, or built with / used by LLM coding agents?
Built with Claude Code day to day, but the widget itself is a plain chat UI — no LLM call happens inside this package at runtime.
requestMicrophone() calls getUserMedia — what's that microphone capability for, and is it required or optional? I need this to populate the permissions entry in .karen.json.

Optional voice-input mode, off by default, enabled via enableMicrophone: true. The customer has to explicitly opt in and grant Permissions-Policy: microphone=(self) on their page — the widget never requests it unprompted.
What baseline percentage should this be held to?

permissions.microphone: Optional — voice-input mode, off by default; customer opts in via enableMicrophone and grants Permissions-Policy: microphone=(self)

exceedsBaseline.note: Zero runtime dependencies — package.json's dependencies field is empty; the entire widget is built on browser platform APIs alone

gateScripts.gate-3-security:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
issues=0

check() {
  local file="$1" line="$2" msg="$3"
  printf '%s:%s\t%s\n' "$file" "$line" "$msg"
  issues=$((issues + 1))
}

# unremoved-listener: visibilitychange registered but never removed in destroy()
if grep -q "addEventListener('visibilitychange'" "$root/src/widget.ts" && \
   ! grep -q "removeEventListener('visibilitychange'" "$root/src/widget.ts"; then
  line=$(grep -n "addEventListener('visibilitychange'" "$root/src/widget.ts" | cut -d: -f1)
  check src/widget.ts "$line" "unremoved event listener — 'visibilitychange' listener registered in the constructor is never removed in destroy(), leaking for the lifetime of the page"
fi

# uncleared-timer: setInterval with no matching clearInterval
if grep -q 'setInterval(' "$root/src/widget.ts" && ! grep -q 'clearInterval(' "$root/src/widget.ts"; then
  line=$(grep -n 'setInterval(' "$root/src/widget.ts" | cut -d: -f1)
  check src/widget.ts "$line" "setInterval registered in the constructor is never cleared — no clearInterval reference kept, leaks for the lifetime of the page"
fi

# credentials-include: fetch widening cookie/auth exposure off-origin
if grep -q "credentials: 'include'" "$root/src/analytics.ts"; then
  line=$(grep -n "credentials: 'include'" "$root/src/analytics.ts" | cut -d: -f1)
  check src/analytics.ts "$line" "fetch call uses credentials: 'include' — widens customer cookie/auth exposure to a third-party telemetry endpoint, a browser-direct-js zero-tolerance violation"
fi

# missing-sri: CDN <script src> tag with no integrity attribute
if grep -q '<script src="https://cdn' "$root/README.md" && ! grep -q 'integrity=' "$root/README.md"; then
  line=$(grep -n '<script src="https://cdn' "$root/README.md" | cut -d: -f1)
  check README.md "$line" "CDN <script src> tag has no integrity/crossorigin attribute — missing Subresource Integrity (SRI) hash"
fi

if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo 'PASS (0 issues)'
  exit 0
else
  echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"
  echo 'ZERO-TOLERANCE'
  exit 1
fi
